spec_match.rationale: Scans code for the seven deprecated getters and reports each call site — directly the lint use case for the deprecation.

interactive.rationale: Real lint+fix workflow: lint button, fix-all button, sample loader, live findings list with line numbers.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Card promises paste-and-scan with replacement suggestions; delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: Lint uses regex on raw text — would lint a string literal like 'loc.calendars' inside a comment as a finding. No AST parsing. Same false-positive shape as the codemod sibling but unreported in the UI.

design_system.rationale: Chip / findings use design system; .warn chip sets both background and color (--accent-amber + --bg-ivory), passing rule #6.

openQuestions.title: Note the false-positive risk
openQuestions.detail: Document near the findings panel that this is a regex scan and may flag comments / string literals. Visitors will copy this into their tooling pipelines if it looks clean.

openQuestions.title: Add an ESLint rule snippet
openQuestions.detail: The page would be much more useful if it shipped a copy-pasteable ESLint rule (`no-restricted-properties` config) that visitors could drop into their config to make this permanent.

summary: Resolved the required ChromeStatus reference; 2 follow-up question(s) remain.
